question.text: Which of the following statutes primarily governs foreign exchange aspects of M&A transactions in India?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** FEMA 1999 governs all foreign exchange aspects of M&A transactions in India, including cross-border acquisitions, foreign investment limits, and repatriation of proceeds. It is one of the key statutes in the M&A regulatory framework. **The Trap:** Companies Act 2013 governs corporate restructuring mechanics, SEBI SAST covers listed company share acquisitions, and Competition Act 2002 deals with anti-competitive concerns — none of these specifically address foreign exchange aspects. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series IX tests the entire regulatory framework for M&A. FEMA knowledge is also relevant for NISM Series V-B (Mutual Fund) regarding foreign investment in mutual fund schemes. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Cross-border M&A always requires a FEMA compliance check — missing this can result in void transactions and severe penalties under RBI enforcement actions.
